etymology.description: semantic 食(食) (shí, "food") + phonetic 圂(圂) (hùn, "confused") -> mix; confused
etymology.interpretation: 餛 combines the semantic element 食(食) (shí, "food") to indicate something related to food, with 圂(圂) (hùn, "confused") providing the sound. The combination suggests the concept of mixed or confused, typically in the context of food preparation.
etymology.simplification: 食(食) (shí, "food") was simplified to 饣(食) and phonetic 圂(圂) (hùn, "confused") remains as the phonetic component.

examples.chinese: 他把所有材料都混了在一起(他把所有材料都餛了在一起)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bǎ suǒyǒu cáiliào dōu húnle zài yīqǐ.
examples.english: He mixed all the ingredients together.

examples.chinese: 我觉得他的想法有点混乱(我覺得他的想法有點餛亂)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ juéde tā de xiǎngfǎ yǒudiǎn húnluàn.
examples.english: I think his ideas are a bit confused.

examples.chinese: 事情变得非常混杂(事情變得非常餛雜)。
